gates.name: leakage_game_pk
gates.measured: 0 shared game_pks across all splits (train=1000, val=299, test=300 game_pks)

gates.name: leakage_pitcher_train_test
gates.threshold: no pitcher in both train and test
gates.measured: 0 shared pitcher_ids between train (1433) and test (214); 0 shared between train and val (111)

gates.name: ablation_overall
gates.measured: 1/3 variants pass (identity_only 94.06%); tokens_only 1.03% and count_only 1.36% both well under 10% gate

ablation_summary.verdict: FAIL — pitcher-disjoint split fix worked (identity_only collapsed 94% as expected, proving the prior 180% inversion was pure pitcher-id memorization). But context features still don't differentiate: tokens_only and count_only land within 1.0–1.4% of full. At 1K train games / 5 epochs / pitcher-disjoint, the transformer context is not pulling weight.

calibration_summary.verdict: PASS — both pre- and post-temperature ECE under 0.10 gate. Temperature scaling halves ECE.

comparison_pre_vs_post_fix.interpretation: Pre-fix, identity_only was 180% BETTER than full (memorizing the 409 leaked pitchers). Post-fix, identity_only is 94% WORSE than full (correctly degraded with no train-pitcher signal in test). The fix worked exactly as designed; the remaining failure of tokens_only/count_only gap is a separate, honest finding about context-feature contribution at this data scale.

notes: Pitcher-disjoint split implemented in PitchSequenceDataset (see modified src/analytics/pitchgpt.py). Both leakage gates now PASS cleanly. Ablation overall still FAILs because context features (tokens_only, count_only) contribute <1.5% lift over the full model — this is a real finding about the architecture/data scale, not a leakage artifact. Calibration excellent. Note: validation_summary.json written by PM after the fixer agent terminated mid-consolidation (API error); all underlying artifacts (ablation metrics, calibration, model checkpoint) were generated by the agent before termination and are intact.
